goal.answer: Build a Python CLI Kubernetes troubleshooting agent that uses LangChain to call a user-specified LLM, auto-gathers read-only cluster state via kubectl, and returns a Markdown diagnosis with suggested commands for pod, resource, networking, storage, and node issues.

payload.plain: Build a Python CLI Kubernetes troubleshooting agent that uses LangChain to call a user-specified LLM, auto-gathers read-only cluster state via kubectl, and returns a Markdown diagnosis with suggested commands for pod, resource, networking, storage, and node issues.

criteria.answer:
A new directory `k8s_troubleshooting_agent/` exists with a runnable CLI entry point.
The CLI requires `--provider` and `--model` and fails gracefully if either is missing.
The agent auto-runs read-only kubectl commands for each supported scenario and feeds output to the LLM.
The agent outputs a Markdown report with facts, diagnosis, suggested commands, and a safety note.
Unit tests mock kubectl and verify each scenario triggers the expected commands and structured prompts.
README documents installation, provider env vars, examples, and the read-only/suggest-only model.
Dependencies are declared in `pyproject.toml` including langchain, langchain-ollama, langchain-openai, and pytest.

plain: A new directory `k8s_troubleshooting_agent/` exists with a runnable CLI entry point.
The CLI requires `--provider` and `--model` and fails gracefully if either is missing.
The agent auto-runs read-only kubectl commands for each supported scenario and feeds output to the LLM.
The agent outputs a Markdown report with facts, diagnosis, suggested commands, and a safety note.
Unit tests mock kubectl and verify each scenario triggers the expected commands and structured prompts.
README documents installation, provider env vars, examples, and the read-only/suggest-only model.
Dependencies are declared in `pyproject.toml` including langchain, langchain-ollama, langchain-openai, and pytest.

constraints.answer: No destructive kubectl commands may be executed or suggested without explicit safety framing., Provider and model must be explicitly provided by the user; no baked-in default LLM., The agent must work against the user's current kubeconfig context and not require cluster-side installation., Keep dependencies minimal: LangChain provider packages, subprocess kubectl, pytest.

plain: No destructive kubectl commands may be executed or suggested without explicit safety framing.
Provider and model must be explicitly provided by the user; no baked-in default LLM.
The agent must work against the user's current kubeconfig context and not require cluster-side installation.
Keep dependencies minimal: LangChain provider packages, subprocess kubectl, pytest.

phases.answer: Bootstrap project scaffold, Implement read-only kubectl layer, Integrate LangChain LLM providers, Build diagnosis agent and CLI output, Documentation and test coverage

phaseSnapshots.name: Bootstrap project scaffold
phaseSnapshots.goal: Create the package layout, dependency file, and entry point so the project is installable.

steps.description: Add `pyproject.toml` declaring package metadata, console script entry point, and dependencies (langchain, langchain-ollama, langchain-openai, pytest).
Add initial `README.md` with project overview and installation instructions.

phaseSnapshots.name: Implement read-only kubectl layer
phaseSnapshots.goal: Build a safe kubectl runner and per-scenario command maps that gather cluster facts.

steps.description: Implement `kubectl.py` with a whitelist of read-only commands and a function `run(command, args)` that returns stdout/stderr.
Implement `scenarios.py` mapping each scenario (pods, resources, networking, pvc, nodes) to the kubectl commands needed.

verification.command: python -c 'from k8s_troubleshooting_agent.scenarios import COMMANDS; assert "pods" in COMMANDS'

steps.description: Add unit tests in `tests/test_kubectl.py` verifying the whitelist rejects destructive verbs and scenario commands are generated.

phaseSnapshots.name: Integrate LangChain LLM providers
phaseSnapshots.goal: Support user-specified Ollama and OpenAI providers through a factory function.

steps.description: Implement `llm.py` with `build_llm(provider, model)` returning a LangChain chat model, validating required env vars.
Wire `--provider` and `--model` as required CLI arguments in `cli.py`.
Add `tests/test_llm.py` to verify factory behavior for supported providers and missing-provider errors.

phaseSnapshots.name: Build diagnosis agent and CLI output
phaseSnapshots.goal: Assemble cluster facts, prompt the LLM, and render a Markdown diagnosis with suggested commands.

steps.description: Implement `prompts.py` with a system prompt that enforces read-only, suggest-only behavior and a structured user prompt template.

verification.command: python -c 'from k8s_troubleshooting_agent.prompts import DIAGNOSIS_PROMPT; assert "suggest" in DIAGNOSIS_PROMPT'

steps.description: Implement `agent.py` orchestrating data gathering, LLM invocation, and Markdown report formatting.
Implement the main CLI flow in `cli.py` that accepts scenario/question and prints the Markdown report.

verification.command: python -m k8s_troubleshooting_agent.cli --provider ollama --model llama3.2:1b pods --dry-run 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'diagnosis\|suggested'

steps.description: Add `tests/test_agent.py` mocking kubectl and LLM to verify the full orchestration and output structure.

phaseSnapshots.name: Documentation and test coverage
phaseSnapshots.goal: Complete README examples and ensure the full test suite passes.

steps.description: Finish `README.md` with provider env var setup, one example per scenario, and safety disclaimer.
Add `tests/test_cli.py` covering missing required args and happy-path invocation with mocks.
Run the complete test suite and verify installation works in editable mode.

assumptions.answer: User has a working `kubectl` binary and valid kubeconfig in the runtime environment.; Existing project convention is Python 3.10+ and a dedicated subdirectory per project (e.g., `chatgpt_like_app/`, `RAG/`).; Ollama and OpenAI are the initial LangChain providers; Anthropic can be added later without structural change.; The agent is read-only/suggest-only by design; remediation is out of scope for this ferment.
